sourceMovie.title: Tully
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Tully works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mrs. Doubtfire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Tully clicked for you, Mrs. Doubtfire (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Tully?
faq.answer: Try Mrs. Doubtfire, The Switch, The Meyerowitz Stories (New and Selected)—each shares DNA with Tully while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Tully?
faq.answer: Tully (2018) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Tully worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
